title: BSP DMA mapping and pre_req cookie audit
scope: Safari/Gmail visible state for latest plain-text resend
observations: The BSP has pre_req/post_req hooks and COOKIE_PRE_MAPPED/COOKIE_MAPPED states, but they feed the same dma_map_sg direction and scatterlist checks used by the request-time map path.
The BSP requires dma_map_sg to return a nonzero length and warns/fails when dma_len differs from data->sg_len; the local proof already logs sg_len=dma_len=1 for the failing CMD18 path.
The BSP validates 4-byte scatterlist offset/length alignment before descriptor setup; the local proof already logs the same alignment data and rejects unaligned entries.
The local diagnostic tree maps DMA directly for the controlled synchronous CMD18 proof, logs the SG DMA address, and has already tested descriptor sync and coherent allocation paths.

Gmail/Safari shows the plain-text resend as a sent message addressed to Ulf, the sunxi maintainers, linux-mmc, linux-arm-kernel, linux-sunxi, and linux-kernel. No fresh obvious bounce was visible in the current rejection-search state.
reason: The BSP DMA cookie lifecycle is a throughput/lifecycle optimization and does not name a missing A733 SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch precondition. The failure remains earlier: IDMAC is enabled with DLBA set, but the controller does not consume descriptor word 0.
next: Wait for maintainer/list/vendor reply or continue only source-backed audits that name a concrete SDMMC0 master, firewall, security, or fabric access bit.
